title: MES v2 Rolls Work Center Training

scenes.caption: Rolls = first station in shell production.
scenes.narration: This is the Rolls station in MES v2. Think of this as the handoff point where raw plate becomes a traceable shell record that downstream stations can trust.

scenes.caption: Advance queue before scanning shell labels.
scenes.narration: Before any shell scan, advance the material queue. That activates the batch and auto-loads size, heat, coil, planned quantity, and remaining count.

scenes.caption: First shell requires thickness inspection.
scenes.narration: On the first shell of a batch, Rolls asks for thickness confirmation. Treat this as a quick quality gate before you settle into a repeat rhythm.

scenes.caption: Matched labels create a production record.
scenes.narration: Once the label pair is valid, the system writes the production record and updates history immediately, so traceability stays current in real time.

scenes.caption: Repeat for each shell in the active batch.
scenes.narration: From here, it is a repeat loop. Each accepted shell increments your count and burns down remaining material with no extra bookkeeping.

scenes.caption: Zero remaining triggers queue advance prompt.
scenes.narration: When remaining hits zero, Rolls prompts you to advance. Pick Yes for the next batch, or No if floor reality does not match expected quantity.

scenes.caption: Follow the scan state indicator.
scenes.narration: Use the scan-state banner as your guide. After label one, the screen explicitly waits for label two to prevent accidental mis-pairing.

scenes.caption: Manual mode is available when scanners are offline.
scenes.narration: If scanner input is unavailable, switch to manual entry. The rules do not change, you are only swapping how the serial gets into the system.

scenes.caption: Advance, scan, inspect, record.
scenes.narration: The operating pattern is simple: activate batch, verify labels, clear quality checks, and keep an eye on remaining. If the queue is empty, pull in Material Handling.
